senses.gloss: further, more distant; furthest, most distant

examples.english: the Far East, as opposed to الشَّرْق الْأَدْنَى (aš-šarq al-ʔadnā)

examples.text: وَجَاءَ رَجُلٌ مِنْ أَقْصَى ٱلْمَدِينَةِ يَسْعَى قَالَ يَا مُوسَى إِنَّ ٱلْمَلَأَ يَأْتَمِرُونَ بِكَ لِيَقْتُلُوكَ فَٱخْرُجْ إِنِّي لَكَ مِنَ ٱلنَّاصِحِينَ
examples.english: And there came a man, rushing from the farthest end of the city. He said, “O Moses! The chiefs are actually conspiring against you to put you to death, so leave ˹the city˺. I really advise you ˹to do so˺.”

senses.gloss: to remove, send or take far away, drag away, drive away, dismiss
